opp.name: Axens_Supplier+Invoicing_March26
opp.ais_why:
• No Avoma recording is available for Axens; only a discovery has been scheduled (April 17).
• Scope is Supplier Management plus Invoicing, with no AI-specific framing in the Salesforce description or next steps.
• Created in March 2026 and still pre-discovery, so any AI excitement is unproven.

items.quote: X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c: null; no Champion contact role

metrics.narrative: Business problem is documented in Salesforce but not quantified with buyer-stated financials. Customer_Business_Problem__c describes fragmented procurement data across multiple entities and ERPs (SAP plus non-SAP systems), lack of centralized spend visibility, limited current tooling (only SAP, no advanced procurement suite), need to identify inconsistencies and improve upstream procurement processes, and manual effort with dependency on external SAP team in France. Business_Objectives__c names improved spend visibility across global entities, standardized procurement processes, enhanced supplier risk management, and data-driven decision-making. Value_to_Customer__c lists unified spend view, cost savings through advanced analytics, enhanced supplier risk visibility (geopolitical plus compliance), increased efficiency via AI automation, and reduced manual effort. However, no quantified impact (savings amount, headcount reduction, cycle-time improvement, error-rate reduction, or ROI target) has been stated by the buyer. The qualitative pain is clear (fragmented data, manual processes, limited visibility), but without a buyer-confirmed quantified value case the deal lacks a strong business case anchor. Since no discovery call has occurred or been recorded, we have no verbatim buyer articulation of the pain or the financial impact. The next move is to conduct the discovery call and secure quantified metrics from the buyer (current spend under management, number of suppliers, manual-hours spent, savings target, or compliance-risk exposure).

sources: Next_Step_History__c: Introduction Call conducted successfully 17 March 2026 (contact not named, no follow-up)
champion.narrative: Champion is not identified. X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c is null, Champion_Identified__c is inferred false (no contact role of Champion exists), and no buyer-side contact role of any kind exists on the opportunity. The Next_Step_History__c field states an Introduction Call was conducted successfully on 17 March 2026, which implies initial buyer engagement and a contact established, but that contact is not named in the history entry and no Avoma call recording exists to identify the participant. The account-level contacts include a Director of Procurement, a Director of IT, a CTO, and supply-chain and project-procurement managers, but none is confirmed as a champion for this deal because no discovery conversation or documented multi-touch engagement has occurred. A champion requires evidence of advocacy (introductions to other stakeholders, sharing internal docs, pushing back on competitors for us, driving the agenda internally), access to the Economic Buyer or Decision Maker, and active ongoing engagement, none of which exists here. The deal is single-threaded at best (one introduction call 101 days ago) with no follow-through. The next move is to re-engage the contact from the 17 March introduction call, conduct the discovery call, assess their willingness to champion internally, and multi-thread to other stakeholders (IT, procurement, finance) to build a broader coalition and identify who has the influence and motivation to drive the deal forward.

sources: SF Why_not_Zycus__c (mentions need to evaluate competitors and compare AI capabilities, no names)
SF How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c (describes SAP S/4HANA recently implemented, implies possible SAP Ariba competition)
competition.narrative: Competition is unknown. Competitors__c and Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c are null, no competitor is named in any call (zero calls recorded), and no next-step or task references a competitive evaluation. The Why_not_Zycus__c field states the buyer needs to evaluate competitors and compare AI capabilities before decision, which confirms a competitive evaluation is planned but does not name the competitors. The How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c field describes the current stack (SAP S/4HANA recently implemented, no dedicated procurement suite beyond SAP, internal tools for supplier risk and performance, multiple ERPs across global entities not fully integrated, manual or semi-automated procurement processes), which implies SAP Ariba or SAP-native procurement modules could be a competitive alternative or an incumbent partial solution, and the mention of needing to compare AI capabilities suggests they may evaluate other AI-driven procurement suites (Coupa, Jaggaer, GEP, Ivalua, or SAP Ariba), but none of this is confirmed. Without a discovery call we do not know whether this is a sole-source evaluation (Zycus only, leveraging prior positive experience with Zycus spend analytics per Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c), a short competitive RFP (Zycus plus one or two peers), or a wide-open RFI. The next move is to conduct the discovery call and ask explicitly who else they are evaluating, what their current procurement-tool landscape is (SAP-native vs third-party suite), and what their evaluation criteria and timeline are, so we can position against the competitive set and understand our wedge (AI capabilities, multi-ERP integration, prior Zycus relationship).

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is documented in Salesforce fields but not confirmed with a buyer quote from a call (zero calls recorded). Customer_Business_Problem__c describes fragmented procurement data across multiple entities and ERPs (SAP plus non-SAP systems), lack of centralized visibility into spend and supplier data, limited current tooling (only SAP, no advanced procurement suite), need to identify inconsistencies and improve upstream procurement processes, and manual effort with dependency on external teams (SAP team in France, no internal IT support). Compelling_Event__c names increasing geopolitical risks and tariffs impacting sourcing decisions, need for better spend visibility to drive cost optimization and compliance, recent SAP S/4HANA upgrade creating opportunity for ecosystem enhancement, growing importance of supplier risk management (ISO compliance plus global operations), and targeting implementation next year. The pain owner is inferred to be procurement (given the scope is Supplier Management plus Invoicing and a Director of Procurement contact exists) but is not explicitly named. The pain is operational (manual processes, fragmented data, limited visibility) and strategic (geopolitical risk, compliance, cost optimization), and it is tied to a compelling event (SAP upgrade window, geopolitical pressure), but without a buyer-confirmed quote from a discovery call the pain remains Salesforce-documented rather than buyer-articulated. The next move is to conduct the discovery call and have the buyer state the pain in their own words, quantify the impact, and confirm who owns the pain (procurement director, CTO, CFO, or a cross-functional sponsor).

sources: SF How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c (describes current stack, not paper process)
SF Why_not_Zycus__c (mentions data-readiness complexity, not contracting steps)
paper_process.narrative: Paper process (contracting, legal, procurement mechanics, InfoSec, SI involvement) is not documented. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is null (no incumbent contract forcing function). No MSA, SOW, Order Form, legal-review, or InfoSec-review step is referenced in any field or call. No SI or partner involvement is documented. The How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c field states they recently implemented SAP S/4HANA and use internal tools for supplier risk and performance, with multiple ERPs across global entities not fully integrated and manual or semi-automated procurement processes, which suggests a complex technical environment that will require ERP-integration scoping and possibly an implementation partner, but none of this is confirmed. The Why_not_Zycus__c field mentions complexity of data readiness and pre-implementation effort required, which implies a data-migration and integration workstream, but the paper-process steps (contract terms, legal review, InfoSec questionnaire, implementation SOW, partner selection) are unknown. The next move is to conduct the discovery call and ask about their contracting process (legal review required, InfoSec questionnaire, procurement-approval workflow), their implementation approach (internal IT resources vs external SI), and any incumbent-contract expiration or budget-cycle deadline that would force a decision.

sources: Zero Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor contact roles
economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is not identified. Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c is false, X10a_Sponsor__c is null, and Who_will_approve_budget__c is null. No contact role of Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor exists on the opportunity. The account-level contacts include a CTO (Olivier Le Coz) and a Director of Procurement (the contact name in recent task history, a procurement director role), but neither is confirmed as the Economic Buyer for this deal because no discovery call or documented buyer interaction has established budget authority or commercial sign-off ownership. Without a recorded discovery conversation we do not know who controls the $100k budget, whether procurement or IT owns the commercial decision, or whether cross-functional sign-off (IT plus procurement plus finance) is required. The compelling-event field mentions geopolitical risks, tariffs, and a recent SAP S/4HANA upgrade creating an ecosystem-enhancement opportunity, plus targeting implementation next year, but no named executive sponsor or budget owner is tied to that event. The immediate action is to conduct the discovery call, map the org chart, and explicitly ask who will approve the budget and sign the contract (likely a VP or C-level in procurement or IT given the $100k spend and global scope).

sources: SF Compelling_Event__c (mentions cross-functional alignment dependency, not a confirmed process)
decision_process.narrative: Decision process is not documented. What_is_the_decision_process__c and Mandate__c are null, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c is null, and Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c and Multiple_approvals__c are null. No call sequence exists to read the org-chart escalation or evaluation stages (zero Avoma calls). The compelling-event field mentions cross-functional alignment dependency (IT plus global teams) for implementation, and the current-stack field describes multiple ERPs across global entities not fully integrated, with an SAP team in France and no internal IT support, which implies a complex, multi-stakeholder approval process across IT, procurement, and possibly finance or global operations. However, none of this is confirmed by the buyer. We do not know whether the process will involve an RFI, RFP, shortlist, POC, committee evaluation, or sole-source negotiation. We do not know the timeline (Compelling_Event__c says targeting implementation next year, but no project kickoff or decision date is stated). The next move is to conduct the discovery call and map the decision process: who is involved at each stage, what approvals are required (procurement, IT, finance, legal), what evaluation steps they plan (demo, RFI, RFP, reference checks, POC), and what the decision timeline is.

sources: SF Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c (Zycus-asserted strengths, not buyer criteria)
SF Why_not_Zycus__c (inferred concerns, not confirmed decision criteria)
decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria are not documented. Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, and AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c are all null. No RFP, RFI, or evaluation scorecard has been referenced in any Salesforce field or call (zero calls recorded). The Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c field lists competitive strengths (strong prior positive experience with Zycus spend analytics, AI-driven capabilities, ability to integrate multiple ERPs, end-to-end Source-to-Pay suite, analyst recognition) but these are Zycus-asserted differentiators, not buyer-stated decision criteria. The Why_not_Zycus__c field names buyer concerns (dependency on cross-functional alignment for implementation, complexity of data readiness and pre-implementation effort, existing partial reliance on SAP and internal tools, need to evaluate competitors and compare AI capabilities before decision), which imply evaluation criteria around implementation complexity, data-readiness effort, SAP-integration fit, and AI capability benchmarking, but these are inferred objections, not a confirmed decision framework from the buyer. Without a discovery call or formal evaluation conversation we do not know what the buyer will score vendors on (cost, time-to-value, SAP-integration depth, AI maturity, supplier-risk coverage, ease of implementation, references in Oil and Gas, etc.). The next move is to conduct the discovery and ask explicitly what criteria will determine the vendor selection and how they will be weighted.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score__c is 4.0 with AIS_Status__c Moderate. AIS_Why__c states no Avoma recording is available for Axens, only a discovery has been scheduled (17 April, now overdue), scope is Supplier Management plus Invoicing with no AI-specific framing, and the deal is pre-discovery so any AI excitement is unproven. The Why_not_Zycus__c field mentions the buyer needs to evaluate competitors and compare AI capabilities before decision, which suggests AI is on their radar as an evaluation criterion, but we do not know whether they are actively seeking AI-driven automation or simply comparing vendor AI claims as table stakes. The Compelling_Event__c field mentions increasing geopolitical risks and tariffs impacting sourcing decisions, which could be a fit for AI-driven supplier-risk analytics and scenario modeling, but this is inferred, not confirmed. Without a discovery call the AI fit signal is untested. The tier is AI Curious (they mention needing to compare AI capabilities, so it is a consideration, but no evidence of AI Hungry appetite or specific AI use cases articulated).

items.change: Stage moved from Initial Interest to Qualified (introduction call conducted)
Owner reassigned from Abeerah Fazil to Bailey Erazo

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 17 March 2026, qualified same day after an introduction call. Discovery scheduled for 17 April 2026 per Next_Step__c and Next_Step_History__c. Owner reassigned from Abeerah Fazil to Bailey Erazo on 17 March (same day as creation). Last Salesforce activity 17 March 2026 (101 days ago). No buyer engagement, no Avoma calls, no tasks, no events, and no field updates since 17 March except a LastModifiedDate stamp 05 June 2026 (21 days ago, likely a bulk data update or system change with no buyer-facing activity). Discovery call scheduled for 17 April 2026 is now 70 days overdue with no evidence it occurred. Deal is genuinely dark.

items.detail: Discovery call scheduled 17 April 2026 is 70 days overdue with no evidence it occurred. Deal is dark for 101 days with no buyer engagement since creation. Close date 26 March 2027 is 273 days out, but with zero progression past Qualified stage and no active buyer relationship the deal is effectively stalled. If re-engagement fails the deal should be disqualified.

Zero buyer-side contact roles and no multi-threading. Single contact from introduction call 17 March (not named, no follow-up) is the only known buyer engagement. For a global procurement deal with cross-functional complexity (IT, procurement, finance, SAP team in France, multiple ERPs across entities) single-threading is a major ghost risk. If the single contact is unresponsive or loses internal sponsorship the deal dies.

Economic Buyer not identified, no executive sponsor, and no budget-approval process documented. We do not know who controls the $100k decision, whether cross-functional sign-off (IT, procurement, finance) is required, or whether a C-level sponsor is driving the initiative. Without EB identification and executive alignment the deal cannot progress past discovery.

Competitive landscape unknown and buyer intends to evaluate competitors and compare AI capabilities before decision. We do not know who else is in the running (SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP, Jaggaer, or others), what the evaluation timeline is, or what our wedge is. Without discovery we cannot position against the competition.

Buyer concern around SAP integration and data-readiness complexity. Why_not_Zycus__c states dependency on cross-functional alignment for implementation, complexity of data readiness and pre-implementation effort required, and existing partial reliance on SAP and internal tools. How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c describes multiple ERPs across global entities not fully integrated, SAP team in France, no internal IT support, and manual processes. If Zycus cannot demonstrate seamless SAP S/4HANA integration and a clear data-migration plan the buyer may default to SAP-native tooling or defer the project.

champion_strength.source: SF X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c: null; Next_Step_History__c: Introduction Call conducted 17 March 2026, no follow-up
champion_strength.summary: No champion identified. Deal is single-threaded at best (one introduction call 101 days ago, contact not named, no follow-up) with zero ongoing engagement. No evidence of advocacy, access to power, or active sponsorship.

items.action: Re-engage the contact from the 17 March introduction call and reschedule the discovery call.
items.trigger: Discovery scheduled 17 April 2026 is 70 days overdue with no evidence it occurred; deal dark for 101 days since last activity 17 March.
items.expected_effect: Restart buyer engagement and conduct the qualification discovery to uncover MEDDPICC (pain, metrics, EB, decision process, competition, champion). If no reply within 5 business days, escalate to manager for executive re-engagement. Without this the deal remains stalled and should be disqualified.

items.action: Conduct discovery call and map Economic Buyer, decision process, competitive landscape, and quantified pain.
items.trigger: EB not identified (Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c false), decision process unknown (What_is_the_decision_process__c null), competition unknown (Competitors__c null), metrics not quantified.
items.expected_effect: Establish MEDDPICC foundation: identify who approves the $100k budget, what evaluation steps they plan (RFI, RFP, demo, POC), who else they are evaluating, and what quantified savings or ROI target they have. Without this the deal cannot progress to the relevant stakeholder.

items.action: Multi-thread to IT and procurement stakeholders and add them as contact roles on the opportunity.
items.trigger: Zero buyer-side contact roles on opportunity; single-threaded to one contact from 17 March introduction call (not named, no follow-up); cross-functional complexity (IT, procurement, global entities, SAP team) requires multi-stakeholder engagement.
items.expected_effect: Reduce ghost risk and build a coalition. Request introductions during the discovery call to CTO or IT Director, Director of Procurement, and finance or operations sponsor. Multi-threading is critical for a global $100k procurement deal with IT and data-integration dependencies.

items.action: Position the AI and Merlin story during discovery and assess buyer AI appetite.
items.trigger: AIS_Score__c 4.0 (Moderate, untested); Why_not_Zycus__c states buyer needs to evaluate competitors and compare AI capabilities; no AI-specific framing in next steps; AI positioning is under-positioned.
items.expected_effect: Anchor the buyer's AI evaluation to Zycus strengths (Merlin negotiation agent on supplier pricing, AI-driven spend analytics, intelligent invoice processing). If the buyer is AI Hungry this becomes a wedge; if AI Resistant we adjust positioning. Without this we lose the AI differentiation the buyer says they will compare.

items.action: Surface and address SAP integration and data-readiness concerns with a concrete plan.
items.trigger: Why_not_Zycus__c: complexity of data readiness and pre-implementation effort required, dependency on cross-functional alignment for implementation, existing partial reliance on SAP; How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c: multiple ERPs not fully integrated, SAP team in France, no internal IT support.
items.expected_effect: Demonstrate seamless SAP S/4HANA integration, present a clear data-migration and ERP-consolidation plan, and propose a phased implementation approach with cross-functional alignment workshops. If the buyer perceives high implementation risk they may default to SAP-native tooling or defer the project.

confidence_signals.summary: No confidence signals exist. Zero buyer calls, zero tasks or events, zero contact roles, and 101 days dark since creation. The deal is off track and requires immediate re-engagement.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 17 March 2026, qualified same day, 101 days in Qualified stage with zero progression. Discovery was scheduled for 17 April 2026 but is now 70 days overdue with no evidence it happened. Last Salesforce activity 17 March (101 days ago). Close date 26 March 2027 is 273 days out, but with no buyer engagement in over three months and no calls recorded, the deal has gone cold before the discovery phase. Time-in-stage (101 days in Qualified, pre-discovery) far exceeds the norm for an engaged early-stage deal.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal has been dark for 101 days since creation on 17 March 2026. Discovery call scheduled for 17 April never occurred and no buyer engagement, contact roles, or activity logged since. Opportunity stalled in Qualified stage with no champion, no.

flags: Map the Economic Buyer and decision process during discovery: no EB identified, no decision process documented, no budget-approval workflow known. Ask explicitly during the discovery call who will approve the $100k spend, what approvals are required (procurement, IT, finance, legal), what evaluation steps they plan (RFI, RFP, demo, POC, reference checks), and what the decision timeline is. Without this the deal cannot advance past discovery.
best_practice_check.summary: Deal is dark (101 days no buyer engagement) and pre-discovery with zero MEDDPICC coverage. Immediate actions: re-engage the contact from the 17 March introduction call, conduct the overdue discovery call, multi-thread to IT and procurement stakeholders, map the Economic Buyer and decision process, and surface the competitive landscape. Without these the deal cannot progress.

competitive_position.summary: Competition is unknown. No competitor is named in any Salesforce field, call, or activity. The Why_not_Zycus__c field confirms the buyer intends to evaluate competitors and compare AI capabilities before deciding, so this is not sole-source, but the competitive shortlist is undefined. The current stack includes SAP S/4HANA (recently implemented) with no dedicated procurement suite beyond SAP, which implies SAP Ariba or SAP-native procurement modules could be a competitive alternative or stay-with-SAP inertia risk, but this is inferred, not confirmed. Without a discovery call we have no visibility into who else is being evaluated, whether an RFP has been issued, or what the buyer's preferred-fit peer is. The single strongest current threat is unknown; the most likely inferred threat is SAP Ariba or SAP-native tooling given the recent S/4HANA implementation, but this is speculation. The immediate action is to conduct the discovery call and map the competitive landscape.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is weak and untested. AIS_Score__c is 4.0 (Moderate status) with AIS_Why__c stating no Avoma recording is available, scope is Supplier Management plus Invoicing with no AI-specific framing in the Salesforce description or next steps, and the deal is pre-discovery so any AI excitement is unproven. The Products__c field includes ML iAnalyze (an AI-driven analytics product) but there is no evidence the buyer has expressed AI appetite or that the Merlin/I2O agentic story has been positioned. The Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c field mentions AI-driven capabilities across sourcing, analytics, and procurement workflows as a competitive strength, and Why_not_Zycus__c notes the buyer needs to evaluate competitors and compare AI capabilities before decision, which implies AI is a decision criterion, but without a discovery call we do not know whether the buyer is AI Hungry, AI Curious, or AI Resistant. The immediate action is to position the AI/Merlin story during the discovery call (Merlin negotiation agent on supplier pricing, AI-driven spend analytics, intelligent invoice processing) and assess the buyer's AI appetite.

items.quote: Lack of centralized visibility into spend and supplier data; improve spend visibility across global entities

items.quote: Growing importance of supplier risk management (ISO compliance + global operations); increasing geopolitical risks and tariffs impacting sourcing decisions
Complexity of data readiness and pre-implementation effort required; dependency on cross-functional alignment (IT, global teams) for implementation
Existing partial reliance on SAP and internal tools for risk management; SAP S/4HANA recently implemented
items.criterion: SAP integration and reliance on existing SAP tooling

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Scope is clear (Supplier Management plus Invoicing, Products__c: ML iAnalyze, iSupplier, eInvoicing) and aligns well with documented buyer needs (fragmented procurement data, lack of centralized spend visibility, manual processes, supplier risk management, geopolitical-risk mitigation). However, no discovery call or formal evaluation has occurred to validate fit against buyer-stated criteria. The Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c field asserts strong alignment (prior positive experience with Zycus spend analytics, AI-driven capabilities, multi-ERP integration, end-to-end Source-to-Pay suite, analyst recognition), and the Why_not_Zycus__c field names buyer concerns (cross-functional alignment dependency, data-readiness complexity, existing SAP reliance, need to evaluate competitors), but none of this is validated with the buyer in a recorded conversation. Shoe_Fit_Criteria_Met__c is false, which suggests either a formal ShoeFit/BRD evaluation has not occurred or the fit is questioned. Without a discovery call we cannot assess whether the buyer sees Zycus as the best-fit solution or whether concerns around implementation complexity and SAP integration will disqualify us.

deltas.subject: Multi-thread beyond a single contact: zero buyer-side contact roles on the opportunity and no evidence of engagement with multiple stakeholders. For a $100k global procurement deal in Oil and Gas with cross-functional complexity (IT, procurement, global entities, SAP team in France), single-threading is a ghost risk. During the discovery call, request introductions to IT (CTO or IT Director), procurement leadership (Director of Procurement), and finance or operations sponsors, and add them as contact roles. Multi-threading is the second-highest priority after re-engagement.

deltas.subject: Map the Economic Buyer and decision process during discovery: no EB identified, no decision process documented, no budget-approval workflow known. Ask explicitly during the discovery call who will approve the $100k spend, what approvals are required (procurement, IT, finance, legal), what evaluation steps they plan (RFI, RFP, demo, POC, reference checks), and what the decision timeline is. Without this the deal cannot advance past discovery.

value.detail: Buyer concern around SAP integration and data-readiness complexity. Why_not_Zycus__c states dependency on cross-functional alignment for implementation, complexity of data readiness and pre-implementation effort required, and existing partial reliance on SAP and internal tools. How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c describes multiple ERPs across global entities not fully integrated, SAP team in France, no internal IT support, and manual processes. If Zycus cannot demonstrate seamless SAP S/4HANA integration and a clear data-migration plan the buyer may default to SAP-native tooling or defer the project.
